entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: General term for the vegetation being studied for growth and performance

entities.name: NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Essential and beneficial substances required for plant growth and life cycle completion

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The process of increasing in physical size and development of plants

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested from plants

entities.name: MANURES
entities.description: Organic matter used to supply nutrients to the soil

entities.name: FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Synthetic or organic substances applied to soil to provide nutrients

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality and health caused by the usage of certain nutrient sources

entities.name: SOIL MICROFLORA
entities.description: Microorganisms living in the soil that can be damaged by fertilizers and manures

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to function as a vital living ecosystem

entities.name: BIODEGRADABLE MULCH FILMS
entities.description: Films used to cover soil that decompose naturally and help maintain soil health

entities.name: BIOSTIMULANTS
entities.description: Substances applied to plants to enhance nutrient use efficiency and stress tolerance

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The water content of the soil which can be retained by mulch films

entities.name: WEED GROWTH
entities.description: The proliferation of unwanted plants which can be checked by mulch films

entities.name: NUTRIENT USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of plants to utilize applied nutrients effectively

entities.name: STRESS-TOLERANT ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of plants to withstand adverse environmental conditions

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The overall output of farming activities

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Cultivated plants used for agricultural production
